div.h3: Swap-and-Pop During Iteration Causes Data Loss
Pattern A: Reverse Iteration (O(1) removal safety)

g.text: Pointer Analysis: Iterator points to Index 5 (Entity A)

ul.li: Swap Logic: Array tail (Entity B) is target for fill.

g.text: Mutation Phase: Tail Entity B overwrites Hole at Index 5

ol.li: Critical State: Index 5 now holds new data, but iterator i is still 5.

g.text: DATA LOSS DETECTED: Entity B Update() call skipped for current frame

ul.li: The i++ Increment: Execution moves to Index 6.
Side Effect: Entity B (at Index 5) is effectively invisible to this loop.
Verdict: Naive forward iteration causes non-deterministic system updates.
